config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan সমাজ, karena itu membuka peluang penyalahgunaan kekuasaan dan penindasan terhadap kelompok yang lemah. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan tetap menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dijaga, bukan dihapus demi mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society’s interests should automatically take priority over individual rights, because that is exactly how governments and majorities justify abuse. From a U.S. perspective, I value individual liberty very highly, and I am cautious of any argument that says the collective can override basic rights too easily. That said, individual rights are not unlimited, since public safety, public health, and fair laws sometimes require real tradeoffs. The key is that society should have to prove those limits are necessary and narrow, not just assume the individual must always give way.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Hak individu tidak boleh kalah begitu saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat, karena di Indonesia saya justru melihat bahaya terbesar muncul saat nama “kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sewenang-wenang. Memang benar ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik, bencana, atau keamanan yang menuntut pembatasan tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus jelas, proporsional, dan tidak merendahkan martabat orang. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikorbankan, yang sering terjadi bukan masyarakat yang lebih baik, melainkan kelompok lemah yang paling dulu dirugikan. Bagi saya, kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi harus dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, bukan menggantikannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can become a cover for abuse, discrimination, or government overreach. The strongest point from the other side is that public health, safety, and emergencies sometimes require limits, and I accept that, but those limits should be exceptional, narrow, and heavily justified. I am not comfortable with any framework that starts by assuming the collective comes first, because that can erase the people most likely to be ignored or mistreated. To me, a healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and only restricts them when there is a clear, necessary, and proportionate reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Pengalaman di Indonesia membuat saya hati-hati, karena frasa seperti “kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menutup pembatasan yang tidak adil, padahal yang paling terdampak biasanya justru warga kecil dan kelompok rentan. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan tertentu, tetapi itu harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan hak individu demi mayoritas, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil tetap mengejar kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically outrank the individual, because in the U.S. that idea has too often been used to excuse discrimination, censorship, or plain old government overreach. The strongest case for the other side is emergencies like public health or safety, and I do think those can justify limits, but only when they are narrow, temporary, and clearly necessary. If a policy starts from the assumption that the collective comes first, I worry the people with the least power will be the first ones sacrificed. To me, a free society is one that treats individual rights as the default, not as something society gets to override whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
